title: Focus mode: children inherit focused state
problem: When a feature is focused, its direct children appeared grey (55% opacity) instead of fully visible, making it harder to read the focused thread.
analysis: In focus mode, the app assigns four states to each card: 'focused' (full opacity), 'context' (55%), 'dimmed' (22%), or 'normal'. Previously, both ancestors and direct children got 'context' — both appeared grey. Direct children form the immediate thread and should render at full opacity alongside the focused parent.
implementation: Uses a single focusContextKeys useMemo (not split memos) that builds a Set containing: focusKey itself, all its ancestors via getAncestors(), and direct children from focused?.lineage?.children ?? []. The getFocusState function returns 'focused' only for the focusKey, 'context' for every other key in focusContextKeys (ancestors + direct children), and 'dimmed' for everything else. Since App.css only defines .is-dimmed{opacity:0.45} and has no style for 'context' or 'focused' states, both 'focused' and 'context' cards render at full opacity — making direct children visually indistinguishable from the focused card. The 'context' vs 'focused' distinction is semantic (the focus button shows card-action--active only on the focusKey card) but not visually distinct via opacity. Only nodes not in focusContextKeys receive .is-dimmed.

lineage.spawnReason: feat-2026-016 noted ancestors and children shared context opacity — this promotes children to full opacity.

decisions.decision: Keep a single focusContextKeys Set rather than splitting into separate ancestor and child sets
decisions.rationale: The single-set approach is simpler and achieves the same visual outcome: both ancestors and direct children end up in 'context' state, which App.css renders at full opacity (no .is-dimmed class). The semantic difference between 'context' and 'focused' is used only for the focus button active style, not for opacity.
alternativesConsidered: Split into focusAncestorKeys + focusChildKeys with children promoted to 'focused' — adds memo complexity without a visible opacity benefit since there is no CSS class for either 'focused' or 'context'

decisions.decision: Children get 'context' focusState (same as ancestors), not a separate 'focused' or 'child' state
decisions.rationale: Since App.css has no CSS rule for 'context' state, both 'context' and 'focused' render at full opacity — children are visually indistinguishable from the focused card, which achieves the goal without introducing a new CSS class.
alternativesConsidered: Promote children to 'focused' state — same visual result, but would require a code path change and the 'focused' state already carries the semantics of the clicked card

decisions.decision: Only direct children from lineage.children are non-dimmed — grandchildren and deeper remain dimmed
decisions.rationale: Promoting the full subtree would fill the viewport with full-opacity cards, defeating focus mode's purpose — exploring deeper descendants requires a separate focus click.
alternativesConsidered: Promote all descendants via BFS — too much noise, collapses the focused/context distinction for deep trees

knownLimitations: Only direct children from lineage.children are promoted to non-dimmed state — grandchildren and deeper descendants are dimmed
Ancestors and direct children are indistinguishable visually (both render at full opacity via 'context' state with no CSS class)
No keyboard shortcut to jump focus to a child feature
successCriteria: When a feature is focused, its direct children (from lineage.children) and all ancestors appear at full opacity alongside the focused card, while all other features dim to 0.45 opacity. The focused card's '⊙ focus' button shows as active; ancestor and child cards do not.

revisions.author: claude
revisions.reason: Code promotes ALL descendants (BFS via focusDescendantKeys), not just direct children as previously documented. Variable is focusDescendantKeys, not focusChildKeys. KnownLimitations and successCriteria updated to match actual behavior.

revisions.author: claude
revisions.reason: Corrected three fields. (1) Implementation: previous revision incorrectly described split memos and BFS focusDescendantKeys — actual code uses single focusContextKeys Set with lineage.children (direct only, not BFS), children get 'context' not 'focused', and there is no CSS distinction between 'context' and 'focused'. (2) successCriteria: 'entire descendant subtree' corrected to 'direct children (from lineage.children)'. (3) knownLimitations: removed false claim about 'All descendants render at full focused opacity'; added accurate limitation that only direct children are non-dimmed.

revisions.author: claude
revisions.reason: Decisions 1 and 2 still described split memos and promoting children to 'focused' state — neither is in the code. Rewrote: decision 1 documents the single-Set choice; decision 2 documents why 'context' state (not 'focused') is used for children and why it achieves the same visual result.

statusHistory.reason: implementation claims split memos (focusAncestorKeys + focusDescendantKeys via BFS). Actual code uses a single focusContextKeys Set containing focusKey+ancestors+directChildren (from lineage.children). Children get 'context' state (not 'focused'). successCriteria and knownLimitations reference 'entire descendant subtree' and 'BFS focusDescendantKeys' which do not exist.
decisions[0] and decisions[1] still describe split memos and promoting children to 'focused' state — actual code uses single focusContextKeys with children getting 'context' state
